Mia Ramos: Heads up — Lodean needs a temporary APAC routing + rate window for a customer-facing demo on Tue 09:00 SGT. They asked for 2k RPM sustained, traffic via syd-edge, and their NAT range added to proxy-exempts. Also header pinning for x-lodean-key. Can we do 48–72h window?
Sam Patel: Thanks — do we have an internal ticket + customer id? Need security contact and org approval.
Ava Chen: Lodean (LD-723) sent approvals and SOW. Security contact: natalie@lodean.co. Demo is investor-facing; they need the window 2025-08-12 09:00 SGT for ~48h.
Leo Nguyen: Quick capacity check — APAC pool can handle with 2 burst nodes. Need exact CIDR and whether egress ASN is required. Auto-revoke OK?
Mia Ramos: CIDR: 203.0.113.0/22. No ASN. Please route via syd-edge and add header pin config: `X-Client-Id: lodean-demo`.
ops-bot: Created ticket CS-81234. Linked runbook: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/temp-routing.
Sam Patel: Can we set rate window metric as 120k req/hr (2k rpm) and TTL 72h with auto-revoke and 15m warning?
Leo Nguyen: Plan: create policy `lodean-apac-demo`, reserve +2 nodes on APAC pool, add 203.0.113.0/22 to proxy-exempt list, add header pin rule, set rate-window 120000 req/hr, TTL 72h, schedule auto-revoke. Rollback: remove policy and scale down nodes if any error.
Ava Chen: Please send customer the exact revoke time and monitoring link 1h before revoke. I'll own the customer notification and feedback form.
Mia Ramos: Also capture post-demo feedback: brief survey + whether latency met SLAs. I'll attach post-demo eval.
Sam Patel: I'll coordinate the on-call handoff and set an alert for traffic > 150% baseline.
Leo Nguyen: Implementing now. Pushing infra change,ETA ~90m. I'll paste the curl snippet to pin the header to the route after change: 
```
curl -X POST https://api.redwood.internal/v1/policies \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDACTED" \
  -d '{"name":"lodean-apac-demo","cidrs":["203.0.113.0/22"],"edge":"syd-edge","headers":{"X-Client-Id":"lodean-demo"},"rate_window":120000,"ttl_hours":72}'
```
Ava Chen: Thanks — please cc natalie@lodean.co on the final confirmation email.
ops-bot: CS-81234 updated: infra change applied, awaiting validation.
Mia Ramos: Validation OK from Lodean: demo infra looks green. Closing loop — auto-revoke scheduled for 72h from now. :white_check_mark:

Rachel Kim: Heads up: we’re doing proactive outreach to enterprise accounts re: Hot-route capacity protection (priority routing + tier-aware quotas) now that GA is live in us-east + eu-west. Want to align on (1) what to say, (2) which metrics we can share, (3) who owns escalations. Dropping a draft talk track below — please nitpick.

Rachel Kim: Talk track (CSM/TAM):
- “We rolled out capacity protection on the highest-traffic API routes so your enterprise traffic is insulated from best-effort bursts/noisy neighbor.”
- “Under fleet pressure you may see more *explicit* 429s for non-enterprise workloads; enterprise traffic should maintain SLO behavior more consistently.”
- “429s now have reason codes + headers so you can distinguish quota vs overload/backpressure and tune retries.”
- “No change to your contract/SLA, but better enforcement + clearer signals.”

Rachel Kim: What NOT to say: please don’t promise “no throttling ever.” The point is predictable behavior + preserving enterprise SLO budgets.

Liam O'Rourke: +1. For outreach, should we call it “priority routing” or “capacity protection”? Some customers get nervous about “priority” implying others get degraded.

Nikhil Sharma: Prefer “capacity protection” externally. We can mention “enterprise traffic is prioritized during overload” if pressed, but avoid framing it as “we’re slowing other customers for you.”

Elliot Price: Docs wording is aligned on “capacity protection” + “tier-aware limits.” If folks need a link to send: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/hosted-api/rate-limits#overload-protection (this is the new 429 reason code section).

Dev Patel: On the Support side, we already updated macros to map the new 429 reason codes. Biggest CS ask is: what do we tell customers to do when they see 429? Answer depends on header + reason.

Dev Patel: Quick snippet you can share with customers:
- If `429` + `x-rw-limit-reason=quota`: you’re at your configured window. Retry after `Retry-After`, or request a limit increase.
- If `429` + `x-rw-limit-reason=overload`: backoff with jitter, reduce concurrency, and respect `Retry-After` (it might be short). This is fleet-pressure shedding.

Owen Phillips: Also: if they’re streaming, we fixed termination semantics last week. If anyone reports partial streams ending weirdly, ask for request IDs + `x-rw-shed-reason` and escalate.

Rachel Kim: Great. Metrics: what can we safely share without opening a can of worms? I’m thinking aggregated + customer-scoped, not fleet-wide.

Rafael Mendes: For enterprise outreach, safe + useful metrics (customer-scoped):
- Success rate (2xx) + 5xx rate for protected routes
- p95/p99 latency for protected routes (esp /v1/chat/completions)
- 429 rate broken down by reason code (quota vs overload)
- “shed events” count (if they’re on best-effort endpoints) — but only if customer asks; otherwise we’ll cause confusion
- A before/after “during burst windows” comparison (we have a template chart)

Rafael Mendes: What NOT to share: raw queue depth, GPU saturation, region capacity numbers. Those are internal + easy to misinterpret.

Liam O'Rourke: Do we have a canned chart? If I’m sending an email, a screenshot from a dashboard is easiest.

Rafael Mendes: Yep. Internal dashboard has a customer filter + route group. CSM-friendly view is in the Console backend (internal-only). If you DM me the account IDs, I can pull a PDF export.

Rachel Kim: Escalation owner mapping — proposed:
1) Customer “why 429?” / “what does this header mean?” -> Support (Dev/Owen)
2) Customer says “we’re seeing enterprise 429s / enterprise latency SLO miss” -> SRE (Rafael) + TAM/CSM on thread
3) Customer requests higher burst / exception -> Solutions (Markus/Simran) + Product (Nikhil) for approval + SRE signoff
4) Suspected bug in reason codes / gateway behavior -> Eng (Amara) via Support escalation

Rachel Kim: Does that look right?

Dev Patel: +1. One tweak: for #2 please route through Support escalation first so we can collect request IDs + normalize the report. We’ll page SRE if it looks real.

Owen Phillips: Agree. Ask them for:
- `x-request-id`
- route + region
- 10–20 min time window
- sample response headers: `Retry-After`, `x-rw-limit-reason`, `x-rw-policy-id` (if present)
Then we can triage quickly.

Markus Klein: For exception requests: we need a short intake template or we’ll drown. Required fields:
- requested RPS + burst + duration
- routes + models
- typical prompt/ctx length distribution
- what’s changing (launch, backfill job, etc.)
- confirmation they can implement retry/backoff
I can own the intake + initial risk assessment.

Simran Kaur: I’ll partner with Markus. Also remind customers: if they’re doing batch jobs, schedule them off-peak or use async where possible. A lot of “need higher burst” is really a client concurrency issue.

Nikhil Sharma: On approvals: anything above default enterprise burst for protected routes needs Product + SRE signoff. We’ve had “temporary exceptions” become permanent.

Rachel Kim: Noted. I’ll add that as a hard rule.

Liam O'Rourke: For the outreach email, here’s a draft I can reuse. Feedback welcome:

Liam O'Rourke: 
Subject: Update: Hosted API capacity protection for enterprise routes

Hi <name>,

We’ve rolled out capacity protection on our highest-traffic Hosted API routes to improve stability during regional/fleet pressure. In practice this means:
- More consistent latency/availability for enterprise traffic on protected routes
- Clearer, more actionable 429 responses when throttling occurs (reason codes + Retry-After)

If helpful, we can share an aggregate view of your recent request outcomes (p95/p99 latency, success/5xx, and 429 breakdown by reason).

No action is required, but please ensure your client respects Retry-After and uses exponential backoff with jitter on 429.

Best,
<signature>

Rachel Kim: Looks good. Add one line: “If you see an increase in 429s, it’s typically best-effort traffic being throttled earlier to preserve enterprise budgets.” (without implying we’re hurting them). Maybe: “to prevent fleet-wide degradation.”

Elliot Price: Also add link to docs for reason codes + retry guidance. (Some customers will forward internally; saves us time.)

Dev Patel: +1. And pls don’t attach internal dashboards. If you want a screenshot, use the pre-approved template chart (no fleet signals, no other tenants).

Rafael Mendes: One more nuance: enterprise requests *can* be shed if the customer is violating configured quotas or if the route is in strict admission mode due to extreme conditions. So wording should be “significantly more resilient,” not “guaranteed never.”

Rachel Kim: Got it. I’ll send a final outreach kit to the CS distro list with:
- approved language
- metrics we can share
- exception intake template
- escalation map + who to @
Thanks all. :thumbsup:

Owen Phillips: (reaction) :thumbsup:

Markus Klein: (reaction) :white_check_mark:

Liam O'Rourke: I’ll start with Proxima + Acme this afternoon once I have the kit. Will loop you all if there’s pushback.

Rachel Kim: Perfect. If anyone gets the “we need SLA wording updated” request, ping me + Nikhil — we’ll keep it consistent with contracts.

source.text:
Maya Chen: Heads-up — Aurelia Health renewal in ~45 days. We're seeing a ~60% drop in weekly API calls since Monday. Main technical POC (former CTO) left two weeks ago. AE mentioned a competitor trial. Need quick triage and an outreach plan. :eyes:
Jordan Lee: Confirmed. Had a 1:1 with their AE — procurement is talking to Nimbus AI for a narrow use-case. Not full churn signal but seat activity is down, esp on the `reporting` endpoint. Dashboard: https://redwood.app/d/abc123/aurelia-usage
Ethan Park: Can you paste the query you used? I'll pull token-level metrics and error logs for the last 14 days.
Maya Chen: SQL I ran:```sql
SELECT date, SUM(api_calls) AS calls, SUM(errors) AS errs
FROM usage_events
WHERE customer = 'aurelia_health'
GROUP BY date
ORDER BY date DESC
LIMIT 14;
```
Ethan Park: Pulled logs. Notes: generation calls down 63%, embeddings steady. Error rate rose 0.2% -> 1.5%, mostly timeouts/429s from us-east-1. A lot of retries showing fallbacks to smaller model variants. Could be routing congestion + their traffic pattern changed.
Priya Raman: If embeddings are stable but gen is down, could be front-end toggles or a product change. Playbook suggestion: immediate CSM check-in + offer a 1-week dedicated burst to validate latency (no big discount, just reserved capacity). Also recommend Solutions Eng do a shadow session.
Jordan Lee: AE wants to avoid deep discounts. Proposed: 2-week dedicated trial (narrow scope) + CSM-led enablement + short QBR with new stakeholders (interim CTO + Head of Product). I'll ask to schedule 30 min this week.
Maya Chen: I'll draft outreach. Can someone create a 1-page enablement checklist and a 30-min QBR agenda? Also need a Solutions Eng request (cc @Priya).
Priya Raman: Quick checklist: - Confirm endpoints & routing; - Share representative prompts & expected latency; - Run one shadow inference session with SE; - Evaluate model variants / quantization profile; - Verify SSO & compliance docs. QBR agenda (30m): 1) Current usage + goals (10m) 2) Latency/cost tradeoffs (8m) 3) Action plan: trial + owners (8m) 4) Quick next steps (4m).
Jordan Lee: Procurement also asked about SSO and data residency. AE will prep compliance packet. CS-Notifier: renewal playbook triggered for Aurelia Health & reminder scheduled.
CS-Notifier: Reminder: Aurelia Health renewal in 45 days — renewal playbook active. Owner: Jordan Lee.
Maya Chen: Thanks — I'll reach out now with a soft touch and propose the 30-min QBR. @Ethan can you keep digging on the 429 spikes and possible routing fallbacks? @Priya please file the SE request and flag availability. Will update this thread after QBR booking.

Rachel Kim: Quick status + next steps for Helios Defense air-gapped pilot (signed offline bundles). Want to make sure we’re aligned before we book the next checkpoint w/ their platform + security folks.

Markus Klein: thx. last I saw from Colin/Owen: installer preflight failed on verification at first, then we got it passing w/ a new bundle? what’s the current state

Colin O'Donnell: current state as of this morning: 
- They can run `installer bundle inspect` and it shows the bundle contents + manifest fine.
- Signature verification passes on the bastion host.
- Install still blocked in the actual enclave because they’re using the older installer binary (1.12.3) that doesn’t understand detached sig layout.

Owen Phillips: yup. their flow is basically: USB -> staging VM -> unzip -> scp into enclave. the first issue was hash mismatch after transfer (looks like unzip tooling on staging VM was rewriting line endings in one of the small text artifacts in /manifests). we switched them to using `tar --no-same-owner` from our bundle, that part went away.

Fatima Noor: +1. this matches the constraint they told us: they have “approved” decompression tooling only (some older distro packages). we should bake that into the pilot runbook recommendations.

Bruno Silva: FYI I can join their next working session and walk them through using the new installer build. If we can’t get the new installer in, we’re stuck.

Rachel Kim: Great. For blockers, I’m tracking:
1) Installer version mismatch in enclave (needs >= 1.13.0 for detached signature + trust store)
2) Offline transfer integrity / tooling guidance (tar vs zip, avoid auto-normalization)
3) Time skew risk (they mentioned NTP is blocked; if they rebuild a VM image we could see “not yet valid” again)

Dev Patel: from Support side, we should make sure they can capture logs in a way we can consume w/o phone-a-friend. did they generate a support bundle yet w/ the new metadata?

Owen Phillips: not yet. once they use the newer installer, the support bundle includes signature verification output + bundle build id. right now their support bundle doesn’t include any of the signing context.

Ethan Park: installer ask is real — 1.12.x won’t parse `manifest.sig` and will fall back to “unsigned bundle” path (then fail closed because air-gapped mode). We can provide a single static binary + checksum for 1.13.1 and they can import it like any other artifact.

Vivek Kulkarni: For pilot success criteria: we should be explicit w/ Helios on what “done” looks like. I’d propose:
- Verify signature + manifest hashes offline in enclave
- Complete install end-to-end with signed bundle
- Demonstrate upgrade to next patch bundle (or at least dry-run verify) 
- Confirm local audit events are generated and can be exported (even if they don’t enable export yet)

Colin O'Donnell: agreed. also they asked about “who signs the key” / proof for auditors. we should point them to the key ceremony writeup and explain trust store update cadence. they don’t need customer-signed bundles for pilot.

Fatima Noor: re their security team: they’ll want a clean explanation:
- what’s signed (manifest)
- what’s hashed (every artifact)
- what breaks install
- break-glass exists but is policy-gated + noisy in audit logs
We should prep that as bullets for the checkpoint.

Markus Klein: scheduling: they’re expecting a checkpoint next week right? who are the Helios attendees

Rachel Kim: Helios list from last call: Priya (Platform Eng lead), Samir (Security Eng), and their Ops manager (Elaine). They asked for 45 min.

Bruno Silva: I’m available Tue 11:00–13:00 PT or Wed 09:00–11:00 PT. Prefer Tue so we have runway if the installer import gets stuck.

Colin O'Donnell: Tue works. also I want Ethan there for 10 min to confirm the installer requirement and the detached sig behavior. otherwise we’ll get stuck in “can we use the old one?” loop.

Ethan Park: I can do Tue 11:30 PT for first 15 min.

Dev Patel: pls also include Owen or me. if this turns into a “what logs do you need” situation, better to answer live.

Owen Phillips: I can join.

Rachel Kim: ok I’ll propose Tue 11:30 PT (45 min). Agenda draft:
1) Pilot status recap (what’s working, what’s not)
2) Installer upgrade plan for enclave (1.13.1 binary + checksum + import steps)
3) Transfer tooling recommendation (tar flow) + integrity checks
4) Time skew mitigation (what to do if they see not-yet-valid; confirm their time source)
5) Success criteria + next milestone (upgrade/dry run)

Fatima Noor: add 2 min on audit events: where they land locally, how to export later. they asked if anything “phones home” (answer: no, local only unless configured).

Rachel Kim: good call.

Markus Klein: action items?

Rachel Kim: Actions:
- Ethan: send me the exact installer version requirement + the checksum command we want them to use (sha256) so I can include in email
- Colin/Owen: send me the “tar only” transfer steps that worked + what NOT to do (zip/unzip)
- Bruno: be ready to run the live working session right after the checkpoint if they can import the installer
- Dev/Owen: confirm where logs/support bundle live in air-gapped mode + what we need them to attach

Ethan Park: will do. requirement is >= 1.13.0, recommend 1.13.1. sha256 is standard.

Owen Phillips: I’ll paste steps here when I’m back at desk. also note: their staging VM auto-scans USB and sometimes modifies perms; we told them to mount read-only.

Rachel Kim: perfect. I’ll send the calendar hold once I get the final steps. :thumbsup:

oncall-bot: INCIDENT RESOLVED — API latency spike impacting embeddings and generation endpoints. Auto-page to oncall@sre, but CS please assemble customer follow-up. Root cause investigation ongoing, we need a customer-ready timeline + impact statement.
Marta Ruiz: Thanks. I can draft a short email/update for customers hit by degraded performance. Need timeline + severity and suggested compensation.
Jordan Blake: Quick timeline from infra:
```
2026-02-22 03:12 UTC - first error/latency alerts from edge
2026-02-22 03:18 UTC - alert escalated to oncall, routing rule misconfiguration suspected
2026-02-22 03:27 UTC - mitigation: rollback of recent routing change started
2026-02-22 03:35 UTC - traffic rerouted, latency improving
2026-02-22 03:42 UTC - full restoration of normal p95 latency
```
Jordan Blake: Root cause so far: partial deployment of new routing policy that incorrectly prioritized a cold GPU pool; caused queueing and elevated tail latency. Still finalizing postmortem.
Samira Patel: Draft subject suggestion: "Update: Interruption to inference service resolved — timeline & next steps". Keep subject concise, not too technical.
Marta Ruiz: Draft body v0.1:
"Hi —

Earlier today our inference API experienced elevated latency for some customers between ~03:12–03:42 UTC. We resolved the issue by rolling back a routing change that unintentionally directed traffic to a cold GPU pool. During the incident, requests may have experienced timeouts or higher-than-normal response times.

What we know:
- Affected window: ~03:12–03:42 UTC
- Impact: Increased p95/p99 latency and intermittent timeouts for a subset of requests (primarily generation & embeddings).
- Customer impact: Some requests may have been delayed or failed; retries should succeed now.

What we did:
- Identified the routing change and rolled it back at 03:27 UTC.
- Restored normal service by 03:42 UTC and monitored for stability.

Next steps:
- Full RCA and preventative actions to be shared within 72 hours.
- We'll reach out individually to customers who reported failures with mitigation steps and credits where appropriate.

We apologize for the disruption and appreciate your patience.
—The Redwood Inference Team"

Lukas Meyer: Quick update needed on Greenline (DL-2025-0897) — MSA redlines + onboarding playbook walkthrough. The email thread has older times; Greenline asked to keep legal separate from onboarding. Can we confirm our internal coverage + updated slots?

Monica Patel: Yep. For onboarding: we should include SSO + data-handling owners. Also FYI playbook doc is now v2 (SSO smoke-test checklist updated + data retention defaults section clarified). I’ll post the Drive link in the invite.

Ben Carter: +1 separate sessions. Also procurement asked about signature timing — last I saw they wanted countersigned by 10/10, but that may slip if counsel can’t meet.

Ava Nguyen (Legal): I can cover the redline call but NOT Tue 9/30 anymore. Available windows (PT): Wed 10/1 09:00–09:30 or Thu 10/2 11:00–11:30. Greenline’s “high priority” was still sections 4.2 + 7, same as before.

Lukas Meyer: Great — let’s propose Wed 10/1 09:00–09:30 PT for legal redlines (Ava + me). For onboarding, do we still want 30 min or bump to 45 given SSO + retention questions?

Monica Patel: Recommend 45 min. Greenline implementation usually has more questions on SSO (IdP metadata exchange) + retention/deletion workflows. Also I’d like a 15–20 min pre-call slot for SSO smoke test if they can provide a test user.

Ben Carter: Works. On signature target: I heard from Elena this morning they’re now aiming for 10/15 (budget window moved) assuming we can close redlines this week.

Lukas Meyer: OK. Proposed schedule to send:
- Legal redline (30m): Wed 10/1 09:00–09:30 PT (Ava + Greenline counsel)
- Onboarding playbook (45m): Fri 10/3 10:00–10:45 PT
- Optional SSO smoke test (20m): Fri 10/3 09:30–09:50 PT (before onboarding)

Monica Patel: Please include link to INF-4521 in the onboarding invite + note that we’ll confirm retention default in writing (their ask was “90 days default” vs our standard). I’ll add the v2 playbook link.

CalendarBot: Lukas Meyer created draft holds: (1) “Greenline — MSA redlines (legal)” (2) “Greenline — Onboarding playbook + SSO” (3) “Greenline — SSO smoke test (optional)”.

Ava Nguyen (Legal): Fine by me. If Greenline pushes back on Wed 9am, Thu 11am PT is the backup.

Lukas Meyer: Sending invites now + will note updated signature target 10/15 (not 10/10).

Jasmine Liu: Quick clarification needed before we roll this out wider — how are we differentiating “signal-driven expansion” vs “support-driven escalation” when the signal is something like 429 spikes / latency regressions? And who owns first response so we don’t look chaotic?

Rachel Kim: +1. A bunch of our existing customers will trigger “repeated 429s” when they’re scaling *or* when something is misconfigured. We need a clean rule so CSMs aren’t pitching while Support is firefighting.

Grace O'Connor: From the SDR side: we just need a yes/no on “should we reach out” and “what angle.” Right now 429 spike reads like an expansion trigger in the playbook, but sometimes it’s literally a bug.

Stephanie Nguyen: Agree. Sales wants the signal because it’s a good Dedicated convo, but we can’t be the first email if they’re down.

Markus Klein: Proposed framing: treat 429/5xx/latency as “Operational Health” signals first, not “Expansion” — unless combined with sustained usage growth.

Ben Carter: Yeah, the scoring service currently tags it as RELIABILITY + SCALE if both thresholds are hit (e.g., 7d tokens up + 429s). But the downstream routing needs a tie-breaker.

Jasmine Liu: Ok let’s define it in plain rules we can operationalize:
1) Support escalation = any “break/fix” indicator within the last 24h: open P1/P2 ticket, status page incident, or error spike above X that persists > N hours.
2) Expansion signal = sustained growth / intent without an active break/fix context.
Does that match how we want to behave?

David Kim: From HubSpot side we can gate tasks with a property like `has_open_support_escalation = true` and suppress Sales tasks while true. But we need a reliable input for “open ticket.” (We can start with manual checkbox in pilot; automate later via Jira/Zendesk sync.)

Rachel Kim: For ownership:
- If `has_open_support_escalation = true` → Support owns first response, CSM gets FYI + can coordinate comms, but no sales pitch.
- If no open escalation and account has active CSM → CSM owns first touch (quick check-in + qualify), then pull AE if it’s clearly upgrade.
- If no CSM (self-serve) → AE/SDR owns first touch.

Stephanie Nguyen: Works for me. Just want to avoid the “support-style” email coming from an AE. :grimacing:

Grace O'Connor: +1. If it’s support-y we should not send the cadence template.

Markus Klein: Maybe we add a “message posture” guideline:
- Support posture: acknowledge issue, offer to jump on call, ask for request IDs, point to rate limit best practices.
- Expansion posture: focus on predictable throughput, Dedicated capacity, SLAs.
And explicitly: don’t combine in first email.

Ben Carter: Concrete decision for signals like 429s:
- 429s alone → classify as RELIABILITY (not expansion)
- 429s + sustained spend velocity or token growth (e.g., 7d > 2x baseline AND absolute > threshold) → classify as SCALE + RELIABILITY, but route first touch to CSM/AE with a “check-in” template not a Dedicated pitch.

Jasmine Liu: I like that. Also need a quick rubric for CSMs to decide “is this expansion or just config?”
Suggested questions:
- Are they hitting 429 because of plan quota vs concurrency limits vs bug?
- Is usage trending up over 2–4 weeks or just a one-day spike?
- Any recent deploy/change on their side?
- Are they asking about VPC/SSO/security in parallel?

Rachel Kim: And if they are already a paid customer, we should treat “429 due to quota” as account management not support escalation. That’s still CSM-owned.

David Kim: We can encode that as: 429 reason = “plan limit” vs “platform limit” (if telemetry can distinguish). But short-term, we can add in the HubSpot note: “429s observed; check if plan quota exceeded.”

Ben Carter: Telemetry can usually tell:
- `rate_limit_type=org_quota` (plan)
- `rate_limit_type=capacity_protection` (platform)
We can pass only the enum + counts (no request payloads).

Stephanie Nguyen: Perfect. If it’s org_quota, that’s a classic “Dedicated helps + pricing convo.” If it’s capacity_protection, we should coordinate with Eng/SE before any pitch.

Markus Klein: +1. capacity_protection often correlates with bursty patterns; may be fixable by batching/caching guidance (Optimize) before Dedicated.

Jasmine Liu: So final ownership + suppression rules for pilot (writing this down):
A) If open P1/P2 support escalation OR `rate_limit_type=capacity_protection` with sustained errors → Support first response. CSM + AE notified but suppressed from outreach for 48h unless Support asks.
B) If `rate_limit_type=org_quota` AND growth present → CSM first response (existing customer) OR AE/SDR (self-serve). Tone = “saw you scaling, want to help avoid throttling; can share options.” No hard pitch.
C) If pure scale/intent (tokens/spend/workspaces/docs) with no health flags → normal expansion routing + cadence.

Grace O'Connor: Can we get a simple label in the task like “CATEGORY: Support-first” vs “CATEGORY: Expansion-first”? That’s all I need.

David Kim: Yep. I’ll add a task subject prefix in the HubSpot workflow for pilot: `[SUPPORT-FIRST]` and `[EXPANSION]`. (We’ll keep it reversible.)

Rachel Kim: Also can we ensure CSMs are always CC’d/mentioned on SUPPORT-FIRST for their accounts? Even if we’re not sending the first email, we need context for QBRs.

Ben Carter: We can route a non-outreach “FYI task” to the CSM owner while outreach is suppressed. Same signal note, no cadence steps.

Stephanie Nguyen: :thumbsup: And Sales is ok being suppressed when it’s actually a support situation.

Jasmine Liu: Great. I’ll update the CS handoff doc + post a PSA in #customer-success. @David Kim can you confirm when the HubSpot prefixes + suppression workflow are live?

David Kim: I can push it today for the NA pilot cohort. Will ping here once it’s in place.

Markus Klein: I’ll draft the “support posture” email snippet (ask for request IDs, logs, link to rate limit doc) so CSM/Support aren’t reinventing it.

Jasmine Liu: Perfect. Thanks all.

source.text:
Rachel Kim: Kicking off QBR planning for our first regulated pilot (likely Northbridge). Goal: tell a tight story that supports expansion (Dedicated/Private) + gets Security comfortable. Can we align on (1) metrics to report, (2) who needs to be in the room, (3) narrative arc / slide outline? :memo:

Jasmine Liu: +1. Treat this as “pilot closeout + exec-ready expansion proposal” vs a generic QBR. I’d like to walk out with an agreed next phase (production rollout + capacity plan) and a timeline that Procurement can map to.

Liam O'Rourke: For stakeholders: customer side we should ask for (a) platform owner, (b) security lead who reviewed us, (c) app owner for the initial use case, and ideally (d) someone w/ budget authority. Internal: AE (Stephanie), SE (Evan/Markus), Security (Olga or Aisha depending on level), and me/Rachel for CS.

Rachel Kim: Cool. On metrics—what are must-haves that resonate for regulated buyers?

Evan Scott: Performance: 
- p50/p95 end-to-end latency (include streaming TTFB if we have it)
- throughput at steady-state (req/min or tokens/sec) for the pilot traffic pattern
- reliability: error rate + any incidents / mitigations
- cost: effective $/1M tokens (or whatever unit they care about), and what batching/caching did

Evan Scott: Also: “time to integrate” (days to first response in their env), and ops time saved (we handled X% of routing/rollout vs DIY). Those land well.

Markus Klein: For narrative, I’d put “economics + control” upfront:
1) Why they evaluated (latency targets + predictable throughput + compliance controls)
2) What we deployed (topology: VPC/private vs dedicated), what they owned vs what we owned
3) Results (benchmarks + SLOs)
4) Compliance posture recap (auditability, encryption/KMS, identity)
5) Recommendation: expand scope + production plan

Olga Petrov: Compliance section needs to be specific but careful. We should report:
- audit logs: what events/fields they reviewed + how to export
- retention: what’s configurable vs fixed defaults (avoid over-claiming)
- identity: SSO/SAML status (pilot learnings) and any SCIM expectations
- encryption: at-rest/in-transit + KMS option (customer-managed keys if applicable)
And we should include “open items + owners + dates” so it doesn’t feel hand-wavy.

Rachel Kim: +1 on “open items.” If we list them ourselves it builds trust.

Stephanie Nguyen: From AE POV: we should end with a very explicit “expansion ask.” Example: “approve production pilot phase 2 for 2 apps + reserve capacity for X TPS” and “security sign-off on evidence pack items A/B/C.” Otherwise the meeting drifts.

Ben Carter: Marketing/ABM angle: if they’re willing, we should ask for a *generic* reference statement (no logo) about: “met latency + audit requirements in VPC deployment.” Even a 1–2 sentence quote helps future regulated pipeline.

Daniel Carter: Evidence pack: we can include a final slide “What we provided” (SOC2 process, architecture diagrams, sample audit logs) + what’s available under NDA. I can prep a checklist so we don’t accidentally promise artifacts we can’t share broadly.

Rachel Kim: Ok, so metrics list shaping up:
- SLOs: p50/p95 latency, error rate, uptime during pilot window
- Throughput: tokens/sec at target context lengths
- Cost: effective unit cost before/after batching/caching + drivers
- Integration: time to deploy in their env + # of engineering hours on each side (estimate)
- Compliance: audit log coverage + export, identity, encryption/KMS, retention/residency notes
- Operational: rollback/fallback story + how we’d run it in prod (monitoring, alerts)
Anything missing?

Evan Scott: Add “quality” even if lightweight: show they ran a fixed eval set and we matched/beat baseline acceptance rate. Doesn’t need deep ML metrics—just “no regression vs current provider/model”.

Markus Klein: + a slide on “what would have broken us” (risk register + mitigations). Regulated execs like seeing that we thought about failure modes (capacity constraints, region isolation, incident response, audit gaps).

Olga Petrov: If we do risk slide, include incident response contact + response time expectations (not SLA language, just process). Also call out access controls: RBAC in Console + audit trail for admin actions.

Jasmine Liu: Agenda suggestion (45 min):
- 5 min: objectives + recap
- 10 min: architecture + what we tested
- 15 min: results (performance/economics/quality)
- 10 min: security/compliance review summary + remaining items
- 5 min: next phase plan + decision asks

Rachel Kim: Who should present what?

Stephanie Nguyen: I can open/close + run the “decision asks / timeline” slide.

Evan Scott: I’ll cover pilot setup + performance results. I can also demo dashboards if needed (but better: screenshots).

Evan Scott: Performance: 
- p50/p95 end-to-end latency (include streaming TTFB if we have it)

- reliability: error rate + any incidents / mitigations

- audit logs: what events/fields they reviewed + how to export
- retention: what’s configurable vs fixed defaults (avoid over-claiming)
- identity: SSO/SAML status (pilot learnings) and any SCIM expectations

- SLOs: p50/p95 latency, error rate, uptime during pilot window

source.text:
Ava Chen: :wave: Heads-up — onboarding intake for ~28 trial tenants in EMEA experienced delayed provisioning during yesterday's inference outage. Several have kickoff calls this week and a subset are in the 30-day renewal window. We need coordinated comms, renewal risk flags, and localized enablement. Proposed immediate actions:
1) send a short status + mitigation note to affected tenants with a 2-week credit offer
2) schedule EMEA-friendly office hours for walkthroughs
3) tag renewals for AM outreach. @Miguel Torres @Priya Desai @Riley Patel please confirm.

Miguel Torres: :warning: SRE update — root cause was a KV cache invalidation triggered during an autoscaler bump. Services restored at 02:18 UTC. We'll have a full RCA by EOD. Impact window: 2026-03-15 22:42 UTC -> 2026-03-16 02:18 UTC. No data loss; symptoms were provisioning delays and failed onboarding jobs.

Priya Desai: Thanks. I'll draft the customer note. Proposed short copy:
"We're sorry — you may have seen a delay creating your workspace. The issue is resolved; to complete onboarding please follow the steps below. As an apology we'll apply a two-week service credit and offer a dedicated enablement session to prioritize your setup."
Thoughts?

Ava Chen: Love it. Add: 'we'll prioritize your setup in the next maintenance window' and explicitly call out accounts with renewals <45 days — offer one complimentary enablement/migration session + credit. Also include a scheduling link.

Riley Patel: I can triage renewal risk. Can you drop the search I should run to pull affected accounts so I can ping AMs?

Sam Kline: Quick query you can run (returns tenants needing outreach):
```sql
SELECT tenant_id, account_manager, renewal_date
FROM tenants
WHERE region = 'eu-west-1'
  AND onboarding_state = 'pending'
  AND created_at BETWEEN '2026-03-15' AND '2026-03-16';
```
Put output in #cs-affected-csv and tag me if you need help parsing.

Riley Patel: Got it, running now. Proposed office hours: Tue 09:00-11:00 CET + Wed 17:00-19:00 CET. Priya, can you host the enablement session Tue CET?

Priya Desai: Yes — I can host Tue CET. I'll assemble a 30m agenda: quick setup walkthrough, common gotchas, and account-specific followups. Will prepare slides + checklist.

Ava Chen: Miguel — can you throw together a 1-2 sentence RCA blurb suitable for customers we can paste into the note? Keep it high-level and remediation-focused.

Miguel Torres: Draft blurb for customers: 'A recent autoscaling event caused temporary cache invalidation, which delayed environment provisioning for some tenants. We've deployed safeguards to prevent cache eviction during scale events and added automated retries for pending onboarding jobs.'

Ava Chen: Perfect. @deploy-bot please post the office hours in #cs-announcements once Priya confirms calendar invites.

deploy-bot: Event posted — "EMEA Onboard Office Hours" Tue 09:00 CET (calendar link) — hosts: Priya Desai, Riley Patel.

Riley Patel: CSV uploaded to #cs-affected-csv and I flagged 7 accounts with renewals in the next 30 days. I'll coordinate AM outreach this afternoon and add a brief note on renewal risk.

Priya Desai: :thumbsup: I'll post the customer note draft to #cs-drafts for review and push live in ~30m once it gets a quick sign-off.

Ava Chen: Great. Quick tracker for execs: affected_count=28, high_renewal=7, credits_offered=2 weeks, office_hours=Tue 09:00 CET. I'll prepare a short exec update and coordinate renewal-team handoffs. Thanks all — please update the thread with any customer replies so we keep a single source of truth.

source.body:
Jasmine Liu: quick q as we roll PLG signals into HubSpot: when do we loop in CSMs, and how are we tagging the motion? specifically want a clean split between “existing customer expansion” vs “new self-serve conversion” so we don’t step on toes.

Rachel Kim: +1. for existing paid customers we need a heads-up before AE outreach. even if it’s “good news” (usage spike), it can be tied to a rollout that’s currently fragile.

Markus Klein: also: reliability signals (429s/latency) can be either “upgrade time” or “they’re hurting”. if AE jumps in with a pitch, it reads tone deaf.

Isabelle Laurent: from ops side: we can encode this as a tag/property on the PLG Signal object + company. question is the definition.

Ben Carter: PRD intent was: classify signals + score them, then route with guardrails. but we didn’t fully define CS notification rules. let’s lock a v1.

Jasmine Liu: proposal for v1 classification:
- “new_self_serve_conversion” = no current contract + no CSM assigned
- “existing_customer_expansion” = any paid status OR CSM assigned OR currently in CS book
- “cs_risk/support” (or “support-first”) = signals that indicate degraded experience (repeated 5xx, sustained 429s, error spikes) esp if they have open support tickets

Rachel Kim: I like that, but make “paid status” explicit: Hosted paid counts as existing customer, Dedicated counts, Private counts. (Some folks treat paid Hosted as “still PLG” but for CS it’s still a customer relationship.)

Stephanie Nguyen: jumping in (saw this thread via @Jasmine). sales POV: we’re aligned on looping CSM for existing customers. for the NA pilot we were thinking: task goes to AE, but task body includes “CSM: X” + a checkbox-y step: “sync with CSM before contacting.”

Jasmine Liu: +1, but I’d rather we gate outreach than rely on task template compliance. could we do “notify CSM” AND “delay AE task 24h” if existing customer expansion?

David Kim: technically doable. router can set:
- `plg_signal_motion` (enum)
- `plg_signal_requires_csm_ack` (boolean)
- `plg_signal_ae_task_delay_hours` (int)
HubSpot workflow can handle the delay OR router can set due date accordingly.

Isabelle Laurent: careful: HubSpot workflow delays can get messy + hard to audit. I’d prefer router sets the task due date + writes a timeline note that includes the classification + reason.

Markus Klein: also: “existing customer expansion” shouldn’t block SE involvement. sometimes it’s an expansion that needs fast technical help.

Rachel Kim: agreed. looping CSM doesn’t mean slow. it just means coordinated. if it’s an expansion, CSM can provide context (stakeholders, renewal date, current plan limits, support history).

Ben Carter: definitions—what’s the source of truth for “existing customer”? billing system has it, HubSpot may be stale. for v1 we can use:

source.text:
Maya Chen: Quick ask — can we do a rapid dump of post-onboarding friction points for the NeonPilot pilot? Need feature requests, pain descriptions, how it blocked them, and a suggested priority (low/med/high). Trying to get an exec-ready one-pager by tomorrow.
Ethan Park: +1. I have 3 notes from the onboarding call. Will paste in.
Ethan Park: 1) Setup flow: customers tripped on the API key rotation UI — wording unclear, thought key rotation would invalidate live tokens. Severity: high. Repro: go to Settings -> Keys -> rotate, dialog reads like a destructive action. Suggestion: add "does not invalidate live tokens" line and an inline FAQ link.
Ethan Park: 2) Embedding batch retry: when a large batch errors, the UI shows a generic "failed" with no itemized errors. Severity: medium. Repro: upload 5k vectors, one item exceeds size and whole job shows failed. Suggestion: itemized error rows + retry for only failed items.
Ethan Park: 3) Billing attribution: customers want clearer per-route cost for multi-model pipelines. Severity: medium. Suggestion: add per-route token breakdown in export CSV.
Priya Nair: Great, adding my notes from enablement ribbon: 4) Docs mismatch: sample SDK snippet uses old arg name `model_name`. Customers copy-paste and get runtime errors. Severity: high for first-timers. Suggestion: patch snippet and add a compatibility note.
Priya Nair: 5) Canary config defaults too conservative — teams hit throttling on burst traffic during demo. Severity: low/med. Suggestion: a "demo mode" toggle or clearer guidance in runbook.
Luca Romano: Tech note on the API key dialog — backend already supports no-token-invalidation, it's purely messaging. I'll file a small UI copy PR if product signs off.
Maya Chen: @Luca can you drop the PR link here when ready?
Luca Romano: Will do.
Maya Chen: Ops-bot, can you generate a short survey link to share with NeonPilot for one-week post-onboard pulse?
ops-bot: Survey created: https://rwd.surveys/NEON-1week (expires in 14 days) :white_check_mark:
Ethan Park: Also capturing a customer ask: "ability to lock a dedicated capacity to a model version during rollouts" — they worried about silent rollouts changing perf. Severity: high. Suggestion: add a "pin version" toggle per deployment.
Priya Nair: I'm going to prioritize these into the CS triage board: High = API key messaging, SDK snippet, version pinning. Medium = embedding retry, billing CSV, canary defaults. Low = demo-mode polish.
Maya Chen: Awesome. Can we use this capture template consistently?
Maya Chen: ```
- Customer: NeonPilot
- Channel: onboarding call / post-onboard survey
- Problem: short plain description
- Impact: how it blocked them / business impact
- Repro steps: short steps or sample request
- Severity: low|med|high
- Suggested fix: product/ux idea or short workaround
- Contact: CS owner + customer rep email
```
Luca Romano: LGTM. I'll prepare the UI copy PR + link the ticket.
Ethan Park: I'll add the CSV export mockup to the ticket for billing attribution.
Priya Nair: I'll share the survey with the customer and ask for short clips/screenshots.
Maya Chen: Thank you all — I'll bundle this into the one-pager and drop in the exec channel by 10:30am tomorrow. :pray:
Ethan Park: :thumbsup:
Priya Nair: :thumbsup:
Luca Romano: :rocket:

source.text:
Priya Rao: @here onboarding customer Luca ran into 429s after switching from single-threaded calls to our Python SDK batch helper. Customer is blocked on finish of integration. Paste of error:

Luca Marino: "got HTTP 429 when posting to /v1/generate, no retry header"

Priya Rao: Logs show repeated 429s immediately after they turned on parallel workers (8 -> 40). stack trace snippet:
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 429 Client Error: Too Many Requests for url: https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
  File "app.py", line 112, in call_model
    resp = client.generate(...)
```

Janelle Kim: Which SDK version and client config? Are they using the global token or per-process token? Also any proxy / NAT that could collapse connections?

Ethan Park: common causes: (1) concurrent workers exhausting per-key rate limit, (2) missing exponential backoff, (3) client re-creating new connections for each request. Ask for their sdk config and threading model.

Priya Rao: @Luca can you paste the code snippet showing how you instantiate the client? also run `pip show redwood-sdk` and paste the version

Luca Marino: using redwood-sdk 0.6.2, code:
```
client = Redwood(api_key=os.environ['RW_API_KEY'])
for _ in range(40):
  Thread(target=lambda: client.generate(prompt)).start()
```

Marta Alvarez: aha — the SDK maintains a small connection pool; starting 40 threads each making new requests will spike concurrent inflight and hit per-second token limits. Recommend using the SDK's async batcher or reduce parallelism to ~8 and enable retries/backoff. Also we can temporarily raise their burst quota while onboarding.

Janelle Kim: also check they're not inadvertently rotating the key or re-setting env in worker threads. If they're in a container per worker, each container might be treated as a separate client and counted against short-window limits.

Priya Rao: Can we proactively offer a config snippet? Suggest:
```
client = Redwood(api_key=os.environ.get('RW_API_KEY'), max_connections=12, enable_auto_backoff=True)
# OR use async batcher:
await client.batch_generate(prompts, batch_size=16)
```

OpsBot: [automated] 1742100060 user.luca@example.com project:acme-onboard - 129 429 responses in last 5m :warning:

Luca Marino: trying reduced concurrency to 10 now...

Luca Marino: ok switching to 10 workers + backoff — errors stopped. thanks!

Priya Rao: great. Marta / Janelle — can we follow up to (1) offer temporary quota bump, (2) schedule a 30m enablement session to show best practices for batching + connection reuse, (3) capture this as onboarding notes under account health? This is a renewal risk if they hit more blockers.

Marta Alvarez: yes I can do quota bump and schedule a session tomorrow 10am PT. I'll add an onboarding note in the account docket.

Ethan Park: I'll open a short KB entry pointing to 'SDK concurrency & backoff' examples. Link me to the snippet and I'll publish.

Priya Rao: thanks team. Luca, marking as resolved for now — re-open if you see further 429s after the session. :thumbsup:

source.text:
Jamie Chen: quick q for the demo env folks — can we reuse the sales demo tenant to onboard a customer POC? (CS team has a prospect that wants to start testing this week and asked for “the environment you showed in the demo”).

Jamie Chen: specifically: can we just hand them a key + a project in the demo org, then swap out later if they buy?

Markus Klein: pls don’t 🙏 demo tenant is “known-good / synthetic only” and we try to keep it isolated + stable for live demos.

Markus Klein: a few reasons:
- demo has seeded fake usage + synthetic traffic generator running (dashboards look realistic) — will pollute/complicate their POC measurement
- model pins + fallback policies are tuned for the demo story; we don’t want customer workflows coupled to that
- we rotate demo keys / reset state often. customers would get broken unexpectedly
- demo quotas/capacity are sized for parallel demos; a POC can accidentally starve a live demo

Jamie Chen: got it. was hoping for speed but makes sense. what’s the fastest “right way” for a POC then?

Simran Kaur: POC should be its own org/project (POC tenant) so they can bring their own prompts/data, and we can set up the right guardrails (SSO later, audit log access, etc).

Simran Kaur: if they need something today/tomorrow: we can provision a “POC sandbox org” in < 1 hr if we have:
- company name + region preference
- who needs access (emails)
- whether they need VPC/private narrative vs hosted API only

Talia Benson: +1. process-wise: drop the request in #sales-demo-oncall with “POC provisioning” in the first line, and include the above. we’ll track it (and we can help with credits / rate limits).

Ethan Park: also from security/auth side: demo tenant uses least-privilege keys meant for screen share + internal users only. we don’t want external user invites/RBAC living there. keeping demo and POC separate keeps audit story clean.

Jamie Chen: perfect. I’ll file the request in #sales-demo-oncall with details. 

Markus Klein: thanks. and if the prospect asks: the message is “demo environment is a curated sandbox for consistent demos; POC is a dedicated environment so results reflect your workload + we can align on success metrics.” :thumbsup:

Jamie Chen: love it. will use that verbatim.

Kimberly Park: seeing a second account complain about tool calling JSON parse errors. Do we have an official workaround we can share?

Chloe Martin: Northwind CX has examples where args come back as plain text (not JSON). Ticket is SUP-1913.

Aisha Rahman: HelioPay had trailing comma invalid JSON when streaming (SUP-1842). We rolled back a flag in us-east but not sure if it covers all cases.

Ethan Park: Workaround: 1) move tool schema/instructions earlier in prompt 2) add explicit STRICT JSON instruction 3) if possible, allow one retry when validation fails. Also we can enable `tool_args_json_mode=strict` on a per-route basis.

Kimberly Park: can someone draft a one-pager for CS? want to avoid each CSM inventing guidance.

Aisha Rahman: Ill add to known-issues page and link it here.

source.text:
Rachel Kim: Kicking off a thread to get aligned on burst beta customer comms + success criteria + escalation paths for the beta window. We’ve got a few customers onboarding starting next week and I want us to be crisp on what we tell them + what “good” looks like.

Miguel Santos: +1. Also need to make sure CS knows where to look when they say “we got denied” vs “latency got worse” vs “billing looks off.”

Lauren Bishop: Thanks. From Product side: beta promise is controlled burst within-region only, with guardrails + clear denial reasons. No bespoke pools. We should explicitly set expectations that denial is possible/expected under fleet safety conditions.

Markus Klein: For SE: can we standardize the onboarding email / slide? Right now I’ve got a deck draft and a few people are editing their own versions.

Simran Kaur: Agree. Customers are already asking “what retry behavior should we implement?” and “does this change our baseline SLA?” We should have copy/paste guidance.

Talia Benson: I can own a single comms template doc + tracker. But need inputs: success metrics, what we report weekly, and escalation policy (pager vs async).

Rachel Kim: Proposed structure:
1) Pre-beta kickoff email (what it is, how it behaves, what can go wrong)
2) Onboarding checklist + required dashboard access
3) Weekly check-in cadence + what data we review
4) Escalation paths (S1/S2/S3) and who to ping

Miguel Santos: For (2) can we require they confirm their client behavior on 429s? Last week we had a hosted customer hammer retries and it looked like a DDoS.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE: yes please. If they’re integrating burst, we want them to respect `Retry-After` on 429 and backoff on `BURST_POOL_SATURATED`. Otherwise they can amplify queueing.

Allison Grant: Observability-wise, we should give them a minimum set of panels:
- burst admits/denies by reason
- burst pool saturation
- their p95/p99 latency during burst vs baseline
- and “neighbor impact” indicator (we have a composite panel showing non-burst tenant p95 delta)

Chloe Martin: Console surface is partial in beta. For now we can share the read-only tenant view + Grafana link. Console should show burst usage + limit state, but some customers will still rely on weekly CSV exports.

Lauren Bishop: On success criteria: let’s keep it simple for beta. Something like:
- They can exceed baseline throughput during their known peak window without manual Redwood intervention
- Burst denial rate within configured limits stays “reasonable” (we should define a target)
- No measurable regression to non-burst SLOs in the same pool (this is our internal gate)
- Metering is reconcilable (no surprise line items)

Markus Klein: “Reasonable” is the tricky part. Some customers will be upset if they see any denials.

Sean Gallagher: Suggest we define: during burst window, if they’re under their tenant burst cap, target denial rate < 1% for reasons other than fleet safety (e.g., misconfig / cooldown / per-tenant cap exceeded). Fleet safety denials can happen but should be rare and we’ll treat them like an incident if sustained.

Miguel Santos: Can we phrase it customer-facing as: “Burst is best-effort up to your configured cap, subject to platform safety. We’ll provide denial reason codes so you can distinguish between your cap vs platform protection.”

Simran Kaur: Also: “cooldown” will surprise them. We should explain: short windows where we intentionally throttle burst admission to protect tail latency, and it will show up as `COOLDOWN_ACTIVE`.

Allison Grant: Denial codes we’re currently using in the dashboards:
- `TENANT_BURST_CAP_REACHED`
- `POOL_SATURATED`
- `FLEET_SAFETY_CIRCUIT_OPEN`
- `COOLDOWN_ACTIVE`
- `TELEMETRY_LAG_PROTECT`
We should keep comms in sync with what’s actually emitted.

Talia Benson: Great. I’ll add those to the customer FAQ. Can someone link the current internal doc / PR? I want to avoid drift.

Sean Gallagher: Latest list is in the runbook draft + gateway surface PR. For now: see the “Denial reason codes” section in the runbook. (internal) sources/confluence/eng-sre/runbooks/burst-pool-overload-mitigation-runbook.json

Rachel Kim: On escalation: propose
- S1: customer production outage or widespread 5xx / admission timeouts -> page SRE on-call + create incident thread, CS stays in loop

source.content:
Rachel Kim: Hey folks — need to lock escalation path + customer comms for Dedicated throughput SLO misses before we roll this to the beta list. Can we align on (a) triage steps + who gets paged when, (b) customer-facing language/templates, (c) when credits trigger?

Dev Patel: +1. Support is already getting “are you guaranteeing X TPS?” questions. We need a crisp flow + decision tree. Also who owns customer updates (CS vs SRE) during an incident.

Sean Gallagher: From SLO semantics side: “throughput SLO miss” = deployment is below committed throughput floor (tokens/sec) over the measurement window, excluding customer-side throttling and unsupported model/version/ctx. That’s what the SLO evaluator will compute.

Rafael Mendes: For oncall: we don’t want every single blip to page. Need thresholds that correlate w/ customer impact. I’d propose: CS can open an escalation ticket immediately, but paging SRE depends on duration + burn rate.

Rachel Kim: Concrete question: if a customer pings CS “we’re below commitment”, what should CS do in first 10 mins? What data should we check before we page anyone?

Aisha Bello: We can standardize the first checks:
1) Confirm deployment_id + region + model + version pinned
2) Look at Dedicated dashboard panel: `Throughput Floor Adherence (30m)` + `Admission control drops` + `GPU saturation`.
3) Check if they’re hitting their own client-side concurrency caps/timeouts (we’ve seen this cause “throughput looks low” complaints).
4) Check if they recently changed context length / max_output_tokens.
If still below floor on our metrics, that’s on us.

Sergio Costa: Dashboard URL for internal: https://console.redwood.ai/internal/dedicated/slo?deployment_id={{id}} (tier filter on top right). It shows:
- committed floor line
- realized tokens/sec (p50/p95)
- queue depth + shed rate
- model warm status

Dev Patel: Can we add a “CS checklist” snippet we can paste into the escalation playbook? People keep asking what screenshots / timestamps to collect.

Rafael Mendes: Yes. Also: define severity levels:
- Sev3: suspected miss, limited duration, no confirmed SLO burn
- Sev2: confirmed miss for >= 15 min (or burn rate threshold exceeded)
- Sev1: sustained miss >= 60 min OR multiple customers OR can’t recover w/ standard mitigations
Then paging policy maps to that.

Sean Gallagher: Measurement window we’re shipping for reporting is 30 min rolling, computed every 5 min. For paging, we can use burn-rate style alerts (fast/slow). But for CS escalation, use “is it < floor for 15 min in the dashboard” as the human-friendly check.

Lauren Bishop: From Product side: we need customer comms to avoid promising “instant credit” for any dip. Credits should be tied to our written SLO + exclusions. Also note that burst is best-effort, not committed.

Rachel Kim: Cool. Let’s put it in a flow:
Step 0: validate whether customer is on Dedicated tier w/ written throughput SLO (Plus/Premium?)
Step 1: verify they are using supported model/version + within recommended ctx/batch
Step 2: check dashboard for 15 min sustained below-floor
Step 3: if yes -> page SRE? or open #eng-oncall thread first?

Rafael Mendes: Prefer: CS opens an “Escalation: Dedicated Throughput” ticket (Jira) + posts in #eng-oncall with deployment_id + dashboard link. SRE oncall decides whether to page infra / routing.
If it hits the burn-rate alert, paging happens automatically anyway.

Aisha Bello: +1. What we need in the #eng-oncall post:
- deployment_id
- customer name (or CRM ID)
- region
- tier
- model + version
- exact time window of impact
- whether streaming
- any recent config changes

Owen Phillips: From support eng POV: 2 common false positives we should call out:
- Streaming token accounting mismatch (customer counting “words/sec” vs our tokens/sec). We should ask how they’re measuring.
- Client retries / backoff causing lower realized throughput than what we’re serving.
Would be great to have a canned question list.

Dev Patel: Yup. Template for initial customer reply could include: “we measure throughput as server-side output tokens/sec over 30 min rolling; we’ll confirm on our side.”

Rachel Kim: Let’s draft comm templates. We need:
1) Acknowledge + info request
2) “We’re investigating” w/ next update time
3) Resolution summary + whether credit applies

Lauren Bishop: Please include the exclusion language in a friendly way. Something like: “Commitment applies when using the pinned model/version listed in your order form and within supported request parameters.”

Sean Gallagher: Suggest exact phrasing (non-legal):
- “Committed throughput floor is evaluated on Redwood’s server-side metrics for your Dedicated deployment.”
- “We exclude periods where requests are throttled by customer-side limits, or requests are invalid/unsupported.”
(We’ll need Legal review for the final customer-facing doc, but internal template is fine.)

Rafael Mendes: Credit triggers: align with what we can operationalize.
Proposal:
- Credit considered when: confirmed below-floor for >= 60 consecutive minutes AND not in an exclusion category AND customer provided impact confirmation (or we can see saturation on our side).
- Credit amount handled by CS per policy table (not in this thread), but the trigger is objective.

Rachel Kim: For Premium customers, do we want a lower trigger (e.g., 30 min) given expectations?

Lauren Bishop: I’d rather keep trigger consistent across tiers but add Premium comms SLA (faster updates + faster mitigation). Credit thresholds being different will be a quoting nightmare.

Dev Patel: Agree. Keep credit trigger uniform; differentiate on response target:
- Plus: update every 60 min
- Premium: update every 30 min + incident bridge if Sev1/2

Aisha Bello: For mitigation playbook (what SRE does):
- Check admission control (queue caps) + shed rules
- Verify warm pool status (did we cold start)
- If capacity constrained: failover to spare node in pool / add node / shift to alternate pool if allowed by tier
We can summarize that in a customer-safe way: “we’re adding capacity / rebalancing.”

Owen Phillips: Please also include a “what not to say” note. We’ve had folks say “hardware issue” too early and then it’s config.

Rachel Kim: +1. OK comms templates draft (rough):
A) ACK / info request:
“Thanks for flagging. We’re looking at Redwood’s server-side throughput metrics for your Dedicated deployment now. To help us correlate, can you share (1) approximate start time, (2) deployment_id, (3) model/version, (4) whether requests are streaming, (5) typical max tokens/context length, and (6) how you’re measuring throughput on your end?”

B) Investigation update:
“We’re seeing throughput below your committed floor starting at {{time}}. Engineering is engaged and we’re actively mitigating. Next update by {{time+30/60}}.”

C) Resolution:
“Throughput returned to within the committed floor at {{time}}. Root cause: {{short}}. Preventative action: {{short}}. If this event qualifies under your Dedicated throughput SLO terms, we’ll follow up separately regarding service credits.”

Rafael Mendes: Looks good. For “engineering is engaged” — define engaged = SRE oncall acknowledged + mitigation started. Let’s not say it if we only opened a ticket.

Dev Patel: We should add internal guidance: if customer asks “am I entitled to a credit?” we say “we’ll evaluate eligibility after we complete incident review,” not in the moment.

Sean Gallagher: Also, for disputes: point them to the same metric definition we use (tokens/sec server-side) and offer to compare their measurements vs ours. This avoids “your TPS is wrong” arguments.

Sergio Costa: I can add a lightweight “customer view” in Console later, but for now CS can export the SLO summary graph and attach to the ticket.

Rachel Kim: Action items:
- Rachel: incorporate templates + flow into the escalation playbook doc
- Rafael/Aisha: confirm paging thresholds + who gets paged at Sev2/Sev1
- Sean: provide final one-paragraph metric definition + exclusions we can paste into comms
- Dev/Owen: compile top 5 questions to ask customers + “what not to say”

Rafael Mendes: Works. For now, operational rule of thumb for CS:
- If dashboard shows below-floor >= 15 min: post in #eng-oncall + create escalation ticket
- If below-floor >= 60 min OR multiple reports: ask for incident bridge + tag me + SRE oncall
We’ll refine once burn-rate alerts are stable.

Aisha Bello: I’ll also add a note: if `shed_rate > 0` and `gpu_util > 90%` during miss, it’s likely capacity contention; if `gpu_util low` it’s likely model warmup/config or metric mismatch.

Dev Patel: :thumbsup: please share the Jira project/issue type for the escalation ticket so support can file correctly.

Rachel Kim: Using `INT` for now (internal support) w/ component “Dedicated SLO”. I’ll add a saved template.

Lauren Bishop: Make sure the template includes tier + order form reference if available. Sales/legal will ask.

Rafael Mendes: +1 include “tier” and “committed floor value” in the ticket body.

Rachel Kim: Done. I’ll post the updated draft in this thread later today for final eyes.

(incident-bot): Reminder: If customer impact is confirmed and ongoing, use /page to notify the current SRE oncall after posting context in #eng-oncall.

Sean Gallagher: Dropping the metric/exclusions paragraph here (v0):
“Dedicated throughput is measured as server-side output tokens per second for your specific deployment, evaluated over a rolling 30-minute window. The commitment applies when requests are served on the pinned model/version for your deployment and within supported request parameters. Periods affected by customer-side throttling, invalid/unsupported requests, or customer-driven configuration changes outside recommended limits may be excluded from SLO evaluation.”

Owen Phillips: Great. For the “top questions” list I’ll include: streaming on/off, average prompt tokens, average output tokens, concurrency, retry rate.

Dev Patel: Same. Also ask if they recently changed SDK version (we’ve seen default timeouts change).

Rachel Kim: Perfect. Closing loop — once I paste this into the playbook I’ll ping for sign-off.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick QBR prep — I’m assembling a tight 6-slide handoff for ZenCart (Enterprise). Objective: combine onboarding gaps, renewal signals, and an enablement wishlist so execs get a single view. Can folks drop short updates + slide owners by EOD? :hourglass_flowing_sand:

Marco Ruiz: Adding onboarding metrics now. Will call out 3 stuck P0 tasks (SAML, initial prompt tuning, billing mapping). ETA 2pm PST.

Priya Patel: Noted renewal signals: usage plateau on Search API (down 18% w/w), two execs unresponsive after last demo, CSAT 3.6. I think this is a Medium risk unless we get a targeted enablement session.

Samir Gupta: I can own the enablement wishlist. Proposed asks: 1) 60-min prompt tuning workshop, 2) RBAC walkthrough + SSO clinic, 3) Cost savings playbook for their data team. Will draft bullets.

Maya Davis: I’ll take Exec summary + final action slide. @Ava can you confirm the ask for an ask-to-exec one-liner we can push to the CSM?

Ava Chen: Yes — one-liner should be focused on renewal impact + suggested exec time. Something like: "Quick sync to review latency & cost improvements that will materially reduce per-call cost and improve search relevance — 20min." Will refine.

Marco Ruiz: Pasting short onboarding snapshot for Slide 3:

```
- 68% of seats provisioned
- 3 P0 blockers: SAML cert mismatch, prompt templates missing, billing mapping incomplete
- Average time-to-value: 18 days (target 10)
```

Priya Patel: For Slide 4 (renewal signals) — quick risk catalog: low usage, delayed payment cadence, missing exec sponsorship. Action recommended: targeted ROI demo + financial impact slide.

Samir Gupta: Uploaded draft bullets to slides (internal link): https://redwood-internal.slides/zen-cart/qbr-draft — please don’t publish. I’ll add enablement session outlines with timing.

cx-bot: Event created: "ZenCart QBR dry run" — 1724775600 (calendar invite sent to participants).

Maya Davis: Consolidated action items:
- Ava: Finalize 1-line exec ask + approval (due EOD)
- Marco: Add metric visuals + blocker status (due 2pm PST)
- Priya: Draft risk slide + recommended mitigation asks (due 5pm PST)
- Samir: Add enablement session plan + prerequisites (due 4pm PST)
- Maya: Assemble slides and run dry-run (scheduled)

Priya Patel: One more note — capture customer feedback snippet on search regressions (copy from support ticket 4432) to use as a quote on slide 2. I’ll paste it into the doc.

Ava Chen: Thanks everyone — this is exactly the format execs prefer. If anyone hits blockers on their section ping me in DMs and I’ll reassign. :thumbsup:

Maya Davis: Will do a quick pass after 5pm and flag any content gaps.

Samir Gupta: If we want, I can prepare a 15-min follow-up enablement calendar template to send post-QBR so CS has a turnkey offer.

Marco Ruiz: Done — uploaded chart images and annotated blocker rows.

Ava Chen: Last ask — mark any items that should be included in the renewal playbook as RED. I’ll track them in the renewal-risk tracker.

Maya Davis: Roger that.

Priya Patel: Adding the quote now and tagging RED items.

Ava Chen: Closing loop — will send final slide deck to execs tomorrow morning after dry-run. Appreciate the quick turn. :rocket:

source.messages:
Maya Patel: Quick heads-up — starting onboarding for Helios Health (HLS-49). Goal: pilot traffic by 2024-06-28, production by 2024-07-19. Primary contacts: Ethan (Platform), Lena (Security), Dr. Ruiz (customer PM).

Ethan Zhou: tracked — thanks Maya. Can you paste the initial success criteria and any compliance must-haves?

Maya Patel: 1) <50ms p95 for API responses under pilot load, 2) SSO via SAML + Okta, 3) Private VPC with no egress to public storage, 4) model version pinned to redwood-3b-v2 during pilot. Also need a short enablement for their SRE team.

Lena Ortiz: SAML Okta + VPC noted. Any PHI expected in requests? That changes our audit scope.

Maya Patel: They said no PHI in initial scope, but may expand later. For now treat as "sensitive PII" — we'll require audit logs and KMS integration.

Devon Hale: I'll own the Dedicated capacity sizing & initial infra layout. ETA for a draft runbook from me: EOD tomorrow.

Ava Chen: I'll run the enablement session for their SRE/Platform folks — prefer a 60m walkthrough + 30m Q&A. Proposed dates: 2024-06-20 or 2024-06-24. Which works for them?

Maya Patel: I'll confirm with Dr. Ruiz and reply by EOD. Also ask them for an integration test dataset (anonymized).

Ethan Zhou: Quick question re: rollbacks — do we want automatic fallback to hosted redwood-3b-compat if Dedicated has issues?

Maya Patel: Yes, set fallback policy to hosted-compatible model with same token limits. Add canary for first 24h of pilot.

Devon Hale: Noted — will add automated healthcheck + canary rollout steps to the runbook.

Lena Ortiz: For compliance, I need their DPA and SOC2 point of contact before we flip prod. Maya — can you request that now?

Maya Patel: On it. I'll request DPA + SOC2 contact and their encryption key lifecycle docs.

redwood-deploy-bot: Meeting scheduled: Helios Enablement — 2024-06-24 15:00 PT. Link: https://meet.redwood.ai/helios-enablement

Ava Chen: Great — I'll prepare slides: overview, RBAC, audit logs, SSO flow, emergency rollback playbook. Any specific ask?

Ethan Zhou: Include a short section on cost controls (batching settings, cache TTL, quantization profile) — they asked about predictability.

Maya Patel: Also add a short prompt-eval checklist we can use to baseline quality against redwood-3b-v2. We'll capture before/after samples in the playbook.

Devon Hale: FYI — initial Dedicated estimate: 2x A10G nodes, 8 concurrent sessions target; I'll put numbers in the runbook and outline autoscale thresholds.

Lena Ortiz: Once we have runbook + DPA, I'll do a security readiness sign-off (quick 72h review window).

Maya Patel: Action items summary: 1) Maya — request DPA/SOC2 + test dataset (today). 2) Devon — runbook + infra draft (EOD tomorrow). 3) Ava — enablement deck (before 2024-06-22). 4) Ethan — cost-control notes & fallback plan (tomorrow). 5) Lena — security sign-off once docs in.

Ava Chen: I'll also capture enablement feedback form to send post-session — short 6 Qs.

Ethan Zhou: Can we add a 2-week check-in after pilot start to review latency, cost, and quality?

Maya Patel: Yes — scheduling note: pilot start target 2024-06-28, 2-week review on 2024-07-12. I'll add calendar invites after confirming dates.

Lena Ortiz: One more: if they expand to PHI later, we'll need a separate compliance path and likely Private + HSM. Flagged for renewal risk planning.

Maya Patel: Noted — I'll add renewal-risk note in the account doc under "PHI expansion".

Devon Hale: I'll post the draft runbook here when ready.

Maya Patel: Thanks team. I'll update this thread after I hear back from Dr. Ruiz.

redwood-deploy-bot: Calendar invite created: Helios Enablement — 2024-06-24 15:00 PT (organizer: Ava Chen).

source.text:
Priya Sharma: Quick ask — can we draft a tiny pulsecard micro-play for AEs to send to customers during week 1? Focus: prefill + small nudge that increases warm-pool hits. Need 2 templates (email & in-app), tracking tag, and a simple signal to mark "prefill success".
Diego Morales: +1. Do we want one for streaming-heavy customers and one for batch-only? Different cache guidance.
Ava Chen: yes — streaming: "start with 3 concurrent streams, enable prefix cache"; batch: "prefill 100 short prompts, TTL 24h". Keep wording non-technical for AEs.
Liam (CS Eng): Drafting now. Proposed in-app template: 'Hey [Name], we primed your workspace with a small set of examples to speed things up. Try <link>. If you see slow responses, reply here and we'll bump your warm pool.'
Priya Sharma: Perfect. Also include a small tracking tag: prefiller=cs_week1_nudge_v1, channel=email|inapp.
Ava Chen: Add fallback cue: if warm-pool hits < 20% after 48h, trigger onboarding task.
Diego Morales: I'll drop a script snippet for engineers to add to onboarding webhook (simple JSON payload).
Diego Morales: ```json
{
  "customer_id":"{{customer_id}}",
  "action":"prefill_request",
  "prefill_tag":"cs_week1_nudge_v1",
  "samples":50
}
```
Liam (CS Eng): Nice. For reporting: add a dashboard row showing prefill_tag + warm_pool_hit_rate over first 7 days.
Priya Sharma: Can someone add this to the shared KB under "microplays > week1 > prefill nudge"? I can sync with AEs tomorrow.
Ava Chen: I'll add the email+in-app copy + the threshold rules. @Diego can you add the webhook snippet to the ops repo and open PR?
Diego Morales: on it. PR link when ready.
onboard-bot: Task created: PREFILL-2389 assigned to Diego Morales, due in 3 days.
Priya Sharma: thanks team :raised_hands: let's keep this short and easy to copy-paste for AEs.

source.messages:
Aisha Patel: Quick heads-up — QBR readout with execs next Tue 10am. Need a concise 5-slide deck + 3 action asks. Who owns slides and who will speak for the exec asks? cc @Ben Carter

Tom Nguyen: I can take the slide on onboarding health. Will add a single metric slide + 2 callouts. ETA EOD Fri.

Marta Ruiz: I'll own renewal risks slide. I'll include renewal probability buckets + top 2 mitigations per account.

Ben Carter: Thanks. Please use the new template: https://drive.redwood/qbr-template. Also we need an "open asks" table we can copy into the exec notes.

Aisha Patel: Good call. Tom — can you add a mini table with action owner, due date, impact (H/M/L)?

Tom Nguyen: :thumbsup: yes. Also flagging that AtlasOps onboarding still has data ingestion lag — noted in the slide.

Marta Ruiz: For renewal risks, I'll mark accounts with >30 days onboarding delay as elevated risk. Will coordinate with CS owners.

Ben Carter: We should capture enablement gaps — what we promised vs what customer can run. A one-sentence bullet per account.

Aisha Patel: Putting an "enablement gap" column in the slide. Also capturing feedback themes from recent NPS.

eng-bot: Reminder: Deck review scheduled Fri 16:00 — please upload final slides to /qbr/decks by then.

Tom Nguyen: Uploading a draft now. Link: https://drive.redwood/qbr/drafts/1715-onboarding-health.pptx

Marta Ruiz: Uploading drafts too. Quick ask — does someone have the latest EBR action tracker CSV? Need to pull last quarter's actions.

Ben Carter: Use sheet 'EBR_ActionTracker_v3' in the drive link above. Aisha, can you consolidate open actions into slides?

Aisha Patel: Yes. I'll consolidate and add a slide with 6 prioritized actions. Will ping owners inline.

Tom Nguyen: Two quick asks: (1) can we include estimated cost-to-fix for each mitigation? (2) can we mark any blockers that require eng support?

Marta Ruiz: I'll add cost estimates where available. For eng blockers, I'll tag @eng-oncall in the notes.

Ben Carter: If an action requires >2 weeks of eng work, escalate to me. We need rough timeline.

Aisha Patel: Noted. Final checklist I'm putting in the drive:
```
- [ ] Deck in /qbr/decks (final)
- [ ] Action table exported (CSV)
- [ ] Speaker notes (1 line each)
- [ ] Dry run scheduled
```
Who wants to run the dry run Mon 9am?

Tom Nguyen: I can do the dry run.

Marta Ruiz: I'll join and rehearse my slide.

Ben Carter: I'll attend as well. Thanks team.
